title: Java Interface

choices: Compilation fails because greet is not declared public in Greeter
completionChecks.explanation: greet concatenates "Hello, " with the argument, returning "Hello, Ada"; interface methods are implicitly public, so the implementation declares greet public.

completionChecks.prompt: Which method must FriendlyGreeter implement from Greeter?
completionChecks.explanation: Greeter declares the abstract method greet(String), so a concrete implementing class must provide that method.

completionChecks.prompt: What happens if FriendlyGreeter implements greet as String greet(String name) with no public modifier?
choices: It compiles because interface methods are package-private by default
Compilation fails because the implementation reduces the public access required by the interface
It compiles but greet throws AbstractMethodError at runtime
The method becomes private to FriendlyGreeter only
completionChecks.explanation: Interface methods are public contracts, so an implementing method cannot use weaker package-private access.
